paper_title: The Ecological Impact of Conquest and Colonization on a Medieval Frontier Landscape: Combined Palynological and Geochemical Analysis of Lake Sediments from Radzyń Chełminski, Northern Poland

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment cores from Castle Lake to analyze pollen and geochemical signals for evidence of medieval land-use.

steps.description: A coring transect was made at the edge of the Castle Lake to identify suitable sediments located in close proximity to the castle.
steps.searchable_summary: perform sediment coring at Castle Lake

steps.description: Samples were taken from the Castle Lake 50 m from the edge of the lake.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples 50 m from lake edge

steps.description: Cores were wrapped in cling film and foil, placed in plastic guttering, and refrigerated prior to sampling.

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing pollen in sediment samples to determine historical vegetation and land-use patterns.

steps.description: Samples for pollen analysis c. 1 cm3 in volume were taken from the Radzyń sequence at 1-4 cm intervals from 24 to 128 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: extract pollen samples from sediment cores

steps.description: One Lycopodium tablet was added to enable calculation of pollen concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium tablet to pollen samples

steps.description: A minimum of 500 pollen of terrestrial species were counted for each level.
The pollen diagram was produced using Tilia version 1.7.16 program.

methods.description: Analysis of sediment samples to determine elemental concentrations and assess anthropogenic impact.

steps.description: Samples of sediment (0.5 kg dried weight) were taken at 2-4 cm intervals from 24 to 120 cm and pretreated with nitric acid to digest organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: digest sediment samples with nitric acid

steps.description: Concentration (ppm) was determined for 18 elements using ICP-OES.
A element enrichment index was calculated dividing element concentration for each sample by the average.

methods.description: Procedure for obtaining and calibrating radiocarbon dates to establish a chronological framework for the sediment samples.

steps.description: Eleven 14C radiocarbon AMS dates were obtained from SUERC.
Calibrated age ranges were calculated with OxCal 4.1 using the IntCal13 curve.
